differentials.name: Left main coronary artery [Left anterior descending]
otherwise split halfway between 1st septal perforator and apex into:
differentials.raw: otherwise split halfway between 1st septal perforator and apex into:

differentials.name: Left circumflex artery (LCx) travels within left atrioventricular sulcus (groove);
differentials.raw: Left circumflex artery (LCx) travels within left atrioventricular sulcus (groove);

differentials.name: Conus artery
differentials.raw: Conus artery (CB) =  1st branch of RCA (in 50-60% of patients); may originate directly from coronary sinus of aorta (in 30-35%)

differentials.name: Sinoatrial node artery
differentials.raw: Sinoatrial node artery (SANA) =  2nd branch of RCA (in 60%) / from LCx (in 40%)

differentials.name: Marginal branches (M1, M2, etc) have an anterior course ›  acute marginal branch = at junction of middle + distal RCA
differentials.raw: Marginal branches (M1, M2, etc) have an anterior course ›  acute marginal branch = at junction of middle + distal RCA

context: arises from left (posterior) coronary sinus (= left sinus of Valsalva) near sinotubular ridge; passes to left and posterior to pulmonary trunk Segments:   ostium to bifurcation = 5-20 mm short stem ›  bifurcates into LAD + LCx ›  trifurcates (in 15%) = ramus intermedius (RI) branch coursing laterally toward LV free wall similar to D1 Course:   within anterior interventricular groove toward apex Supply:   majority of LV + anterolateral papillary muscle ± small branches to anterior RV wall Segments: ›  proximal: from left main bifurcation to origin of 1st septal branch ›  midportion: from 1st se
